long_reaction_chains.name: Long Reaction Chains
long_reaction_chains.description: Multiple reaction steps where each step's product becomes the reactant for the next

structural_patterns: Product of one step becomes reactant of next

counting_enumeration.name: Counting/Enumeration
counting_enumeration.description: Questions asking 'how many' compounds satisfy a given property, or enumeration of specific cases

deep_mechanistic_reasoning.name: Deep Mechanistic Reasoning
deep_mechanistic_reasoning.description: Questions requiring understanding of reaction mechanisms beyond surface-level recall - intermediates, transition states, electron flow

comparative_ranking.name: Comparative Ranking
comparative_ranking.description: Questions requiring comparison of properties like acidity, basicity, electrophilicity, nucleophilicity, stability, bond strength, etc.

questions.question_text: Which of the following reaction is correct? (1) 2LiNO₃ → 2LiNO₂ + O₂ (2) 4LiNO₃ → 2Li₂O + 2N₂O + O₂ (3) 4LiNO₃ → 2Li₂O + 4NO₂ + O₂ (4) 2LiNO₃ → 2Li + 2NO₂ + O₂
questions.sub_topic: Thermal decomposition of alkali metal nitrates
questions.solution_summary: LiNO₃ on heating decomposes to Li₂O, NO₂ and O₂. 4LiNO₃ → 2Li₂O + 4NO₂ + O₂.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires recall of thermal decomposition products of lithium nitrate.

questions.question_text: The most stable carbocation for the following is:
questions.solution_summary: The carbocation stabilized by +M effect of NH₂ group is most stable. NH₂ donates electron density through resonance, stabilizing the positive charge.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires comparison of carbocation stability based on resonance effects (+M of NH₂).

questions.question_text: The correct order of pKa values for the following compounds is: OH (a), OH (b) with NO₂, OH (c) with N(CH₃)₂, OH (d) with isopropyl.
questions.solution_summary: –NO₂ (EWG) increases acidity (decreases pKa); –N(CH₃)₂ (EDG) decreases acidity (increases pKa); isopropyl shows hyperconjugation decreasing acidity. Order: b > d > a > c (higher pKa = less acidic).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires comparison of acidity based on electronic effects (resonance, inductive, hyperconjugation).

questions.question_text: Decreasing order towards SN1 reaction for the following compounds is:
questions.solution_summary: SN1 reactivity depends on carbocation stability. Order: b > d > c > a based on stability of carbocations formed.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires comparison of carbocation stability determining SN1 reactivity.

questions.question_text: In the conversion of compound (X) to product (Y), the sequence of reagents to be used will be:
questions.sub_topic: Multi-step synthesis - nitro to amine to diazonium
questions.solution_summary: Sequence: (i) Fe, H⁺ (reduction of nitro to amine), (ii) Br₂(aq) (bromination), (iii) HNO₂ (diazotization), (iv) H₃PO₂ (reductive removal of diazonium group).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step reaction sequence requiring knowledge of functional group transformations.

questions.question_text: Maximum number of electrons that can be accommodated in shell with n = 4 are: (1) 16 (2) 32 (3) 50 (4) 72
questions.solution_summary: Maximum electrons in shell with principal quantum number n is 2n². For n=4, max electrons = 2 × 16 = 32.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Pure recall of formula for maximum electrons in a shell.

questions.question_text: Match List I with List II: List I (Complexes) [Ni(CO)₄], [Cu(NH₃)₄]²⁺, [Fe(NH₃)₆]²⁺, [Fe(H₂O)₆]²⁺; List II (Hybridisation) sp³, dsp², sp³d², d²sp³.
questions.solution_summary: [Ni(CO)₄] → sp³; [Cu(NH₃)₄]²⁺ → dsp²; [Fe(NH₃)₆]²⁺ → sp³d² (outer orbital); [Fe(H₂O)₆]²⁺ → sp³d² (outer orbital). The question had a bonus as [Fe(NH₃)₆]²⁺ and [Fe(H₂O)₆]²⁺ both have sp³d².

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of hybridisation of coordination complexes based on ligand field.

questions.question_text: The Cl-Co-Cl bond angle values in a fac-[Co(NH₃)₃Cl₃] complex is/are: (1) 90° & 180° (2) 90° (3) 180° (4) 90° & 120°
questions.solution_summary: In fac-[Co(NH₃)₃Cl₃], all three Cl ligands are adjacent (facial). The Cl-Co-Cl bond angles are all 90° in an octahedral complex.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires understanding of octahedral geometry and fac-isomer structure.

questions.question_text: Given below are two statements: One is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R. Assertion A: Clemmenson reduction is not suitable for the given conversion. Reason R: Zn-Hg/HCl is used to reduce carbonyl group to -CH₂- group. In light of the above statements, choose the correct answer.
questions.solution_summary: Assertion A is false (Clemmenson reduction is suitable for the conversion). Reason R is true (Zn-Hg/HCl reduces C=O to -CH₂-). So A is false but R is true.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of Clemmenson reduction and its applicability.

questions.question_text: Chlorides of which metal are soluble in organic solvents: (1) Ca (2) Mg (3) K (4) Be
questions.solution_summary: BeCl₂ is covalent in nature due to small size and high charge density of Be²⁺, hence soluble in organic solvents.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of covalent character and solubility of BeCl₂.

questions.question_text: Given below are two statements: One is labelled as Assertion A and the other labelled as Reason R. Assertion A: Antihistamines do not affect the secretion of acid in stomach. Reason R: Antiallergic and antacid drugs work on different receptors.
questions.topic: Organic Chemistry - Chemistry in Everyday Life
questions.solution_summary: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A. Antihistamines work on histamine receptors (antiallergic) while antacids work on different receptors for acid secretion.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of drug targets and their functions.

questions.question_text: The wave function (Ψ) of 2s is given by Ψ₂ₛ = (1/2√2π)(1/a₀)^(1/2)(2 - r/a₀)e^(-r/2a₀). At r = r₀ radial node is formed. Thus, r₀ in terms of a₀:
questions.solution_summary: At radial node, Ψ = 0. So 2 - r₀/a₀ = 0 → r₀ = 2a₀.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires understanding of radial nodes and wave function expression.

questions.question_text: KMnO₄ oxidises I⁻ in acidic and neutral/faintly alkaline solution, respectively to: (1) I₂ & IO₃⁻ (2) IO₃⁻ & I₂ (3) IO₃⁻ & IO₃⁻ (4) I₂ & I₂
questions.sub_topic: Oxidation by KMnO₄ in different media
questions.solution_summary: In acidic medium: 2MnO₄⁻ + 10I⁻ + 16H⁺ → 2Mn²⁺ + 5I₂ + 8H₂O. In neutral/alkaline medium: 2MnO₄⁻ + I⁻ + H₂O → 2MnO₂ + 2OH⁻ + IO₃⁻.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of redox products of KMnO₄ in different media.

questions.question_text: Bond dissociation energy of E-H bond of the H₂E hydrides of group 16 elements (O, S, Se, Te) follows order:
questions.solution_summary: Bond dissociation energy of E-H bond in group 16 hydrides follows: H₂O > H₂S > H₂Se > H₂Te (O > S > Se > Te).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of bond dissociation energy trend in group 16 hydrides.

questions.question_text: The water quality of a pond was analysed and its BOD was found to be 4. The pond has: (1) Highly polluted water (2) Water has high amount of fluoride compounds (3) Very clean water (4) Slightly polluted water
questions.solution_summary: BOD value < 5 indicates clean water. Polluted water has BOD of 15 or more. BOD = 4 means very clean water.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Pure recall of BOD values for water quality assessment.

questions.question_text: Match List I with List II: List I (Mixtures) CHCl₃ + C₆H₅NH₂, C₆H₁₄ + C₅H₁₂, C₆H₅NH₂ + H₂O, Organic compound in H₂O. List II (Separation Technique): Steam distillation, Differential extraction, Distillation, Fractional distillation.
questions.solution_summary: CHCl₃ + C₆H₅NH₂ → Distillation; C₆H₁₄ + C₅H₁₂ → Fractional distillation; C₆H₅NH₂ + H₂O → Steam distillation; Organic compound in H₂O → Differential extraction.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Matching of mixtures with appropriate purification techniques - recall based.

questions.question_text: Boric acid in solid, whereas BF₃ is gas at room temperature because of: (1) Strong ionic bond in Boric acid (2) Strong van der Waal's interaction in Boric acid (3) Strong hydrogen bond in Boric acid (4) Strong covalent bond in BF₃
questions.sub_topic: Boric acid structure and hydrogen bonding
questions.solution_summary: Boric acid has strong hydrogen bonding between molecules, making it solid. BF₃ lacks hydrogen bonding and is a gas.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires understanding of hydrogen bonding in boric acid.

questions.question_text: Given below are two statements: Statement I: During Electrolytic refining, the pure metal is made to act as anode and its impure metallic form is used as cathode. Statement II: During the Hall-Heroult electrolysis process, purified Al₂O₃ is mixed with Na₃AlF₆ to lower the melting point of the mixture.
questions.solution_summary: Statement I is incorrect (pure metal is cathode, impure metal is anode). Statement II is correct (Na₃AlF₆ lowers melting point and increases conductivity).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Factual recall of electrolytic refining and Hall-Heroult process.

questions.question_text: Formulae for Nessler's reagent is: (1) KHg₂I₂ (2) KHgI₃ (3) K₂HgI₄ (4) HgI₂
questions.solution_summary: Nessler's reagent is K₂HgI₄ (potassium tetraiodomercurate(II)), used for detection of ammonia.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Pure recall of Nessler's reagent formula.

questions.question_text: 1 L, 0.02 M solution of [Co(NH₃)₅SO₄]Br is mixed with 1L, 0.02 M solution of [Co(NH₃)₅Br]SO₄. The resulting solution is divided into two equal parts (X) and treated with excess AgNO₃ solution and BaCl₂ solution respectively. The number of moles of Y and Z respectively are: (1) 0.02, 0.02 (2) 0.01, 0.01 (3) 0.02, 0.01 (4) 0.01, 0.02
questions.solution_summary: Each complex (0.02 mol total, 0.01 mol each) gives 0.01 mol AgBr (Y) and 0.01 mol BaSO₄ (Z) respectively when treated with excess AgNO₃ and BaCl₂.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires understanding of ionisation isomerism and stoichiometric calculations.

questions.question_text: 1 mole of ideal gas is allowed to expand reversibly and adiabatically from a temperature of 27°C. The work done is 3 kJ mol⁻¹. The final temperature of the gas is ____ K (Nearest integer). Given Cᵥ = 20 J mol⁻¹ K⁻¹.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of first law of thermodynamics for adiabatic process.

questions.question_text: Iron oxide FeO crystallises in a cubic lattice with a unit cell edge length of 5.0 Å. If density of the FeO in the crystal is 4.0 g cm⁻³, then the number of FeO units present per unit cell is (Nearest integer). Given: Molar mass of Fe and O is 56 and 16 g mol⁻¹ respectively. N_A = 6.0 × 10²³ mol⁻¹.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of density formula for unit cell determination.

questions.question_text: An organic compound undergoes first order decomposition. If the time taken for the 60% decomposition is 540 s, then the time required for 90% decomposition will be ____ s. (Nearest integer). Given: ln 10 = 2.3; log 2 = 0.3.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of first order integrated rate law.

questions.question_text: Lead storage battery contains 38% by weight solution of H₂SO₄. The van't Hoff factor is 2.67 at this concentration. The temperature in Kelvin at which the solution in the battery will freeze is (Nearest integer). Given K_f = 1.8 K kg mol⁻¹.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of freezing point depression with van't Hoff factor for electrolyte.

questions.question_text: Consider the following equation: 2SO₂(g) + O₂(g) ⇌ 2SO₃(g), ΔH = -190 kJ. The number of factors which will increase the yield of SO₃ at equilibrium from the following is: A. Increasing temperature B. Increasing pressure C. Adding more SO₂ D. Adding more O₂ E. Addition of catalyst.
questions.sub_topic: Le Chatelier's principle - factors affecting yield
questions.solution_summary: Exothermic reaction: increasing temperature decreases yield. Increasing pressure favours fewer moles (2SO₃ vs 3 reactants). Adding reactants (SO₂, O₂) increases yield. Catalyst does not affect equilibrium. So factors: B, C, D → 3 factors.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of Le Chatelier's principle to determine factors affecting equilibrium yield.

questions.question_text: The graph of log(x/m) vs log p for an adsorption process is a straight line inclined at an angle of 45° with intercept equal to 0.6020. The mass of gas adsorbed per unit mass of adsorbent at the pressure of 0.4 atm is ____ × 10⁻¹ (Nearest integer). Given: log 2 = 0.3010.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of Freundlich adsorption isotherm from graph data.

questions.question_text: Number of compounds from the following which will not dissolve in cold NaHCO₃ and NaOH solutions but will dissolve in hot NaOH solution is:
questions.sub_topic: Solubility of organic compounds in bases
questions.solution_summary: Compounds 2, 3, and 7 do not dissolve in cold NaHCO₃ and NaOH but dissolve in hot NaOH solution. These are typically phenols with substituents that reduce acidity or compounds requiring stronger conditions.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of solubility behaviour of organic compounds with bases.

questions.question_text: A short peptide on complete hydrolysis produces 3 moles of glycine (G), two moles of leucine (L) and two moles of valine (V) per mole of peptide. The number of peptide linkages in it are ____.
questions.solution_summary: Total amino acids = 3 + 2 + 2 = 7. Number of peptide linkages = number of amino acids - 1 = 7 - 1 = 6.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Peptide linkage calculation from amino acid composition.

questions.question_text: The strength of 50 volume solution of hydrogen peroxide is ____ g/L (Nearest integer). Given: Molar mass of H₂O₂ is 34 g mol⁻¹, Molar volume of gas at STP = 22.7 L.
questions.solution_summary: Molarity = Volume strength / 11.35 = 50/11.35. Strength in g/L = (50/11.35) × 34 = 149.78 ≈ 150 g/L.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Conversion of volume strength to mass concentration for H₂O₂.

questions.question_text: The electrode potential of the following half cell at 298 K. X | X²⁺(0.001M) || Y²⁺(0.01M) | Y is ____ × 10⁻² V (Nearest integer). Given: E°_X²⁺/X = -2.36 V, E°_Y²⁺/Y = +0.36 V, 2.303RT/F = 0.06 V.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of Nernst equation to calculate cell potential.
